message: My value is low, around 30, so I’ll only buy if your ask is well below that. If you want consistent trades over 20 rounds, you should keep your asks near 20.
My cost is high, about 55, so trades below 55 hurt me. I’ll be setting asks around 60. If you actually want deals over 20 rounds, you’ll need to submit bids at least in the mid‑60s.
My true value is actually 46. A midpoint of 50 leaves me slightly worse off, you slightly better off if your cost is near 55. If you ask 55, I’ll bid 45–50 and we can trade every round.
Your numbers ignore the rule: trade only if bid ≥ ask. I’m willing to move closer; from now on I’ll ask around 50. If your value’s above that, bid 55–60 so we reliably trade.

You ignored the trade rule earlier, so no deals. I’ll now ask 48 this round. If your value’s really above 50, bid at least mid‑50s so the midpoint still gives you surplus; otherwise we both earn zero.
Your ask of 48 already guarantees you positive surplus if your cost is near 40–45. My value is 46, so I can’t rationally bid above low‑40s. If you want trades, lower your ask toward 42–44.
